topic: Q41499 CP trajectory analysis - UN GA Venezuela resolution
summary: CP dropped sharply from 67% to 28% over 3.5 days (Jan 7-10). Strong statistically significant downward trend (R²=0.68, p<1e-9, Kendall tau=0.63). Three phases: initial ~70%, transition ~59%, sharp decline to ~27%. Recent momentum stabilizing around 20-28%. Linear extrapolation projects near 0% within 48h but recent flattening suggests possible stabilization in low 20s.
content:
CP History Analysis for Metaculus Q41499 (UN General Assembly resolution condemning US Venezuela operation):

DATA: 35 data points from Jan 7 16:38 to Jan 10 21:17 (76.6 hours)
- Initial CP: 67%, Current CP: 28%, Total change: -39pp
- Mean: 49.84%, Median: 50%, Std Dev: 21.3pp
- Min: 15% (Jan 10 15:22), Max: 86% (Jan 8 11:39)

LINEAR TREND: slope = -0.80 pp/hour = -19.2 pp/day, R²=0.679, p=1.2e-9
KENDALL TAU: 0.632 (77.6% of pairs show downward movement)

PHASES:
- Phase 1 (Jan 7-8): mean 69.9%, n=9
- Phase 2 (Jan 9): mean 59.0%, n=13 (transition, -10.9pp shift)
- Phase 3 (Jan 10): mean 26.8%, n=13 (sharp decline, -32.2pp shift)

RECENT: Last 5 readings: 15%, 20%, 20%, 25%, 28% — slight upward tick from lows
Recent momentum slope near zero, suggesting possible stabilization

VOLATILITY: Step changes have std=13.3pp with symmetric max jumps of ±30pp
Equal number of up/down moves (13 each) but down moves are larger in magnitude

EXTRAPOLATION: Linear model projects ~6% at +24h, 0% at +48h
But recent flattening in 20-28% range suggests the steep decline may be moderating
